title: QUIC RFC 9000 Long-Header ACK Piggyback Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, deterministic fuzz/property sampling, BenchmarkDotNet evidence, SpecTrace render/check, attempted JSON validation, and diff hygiene for the bounded live Initial and Handshake ACK piggyback floor.
scope: This verification covers live Initial and Handshake CRYPTO packet construction when ACK-generation state already contains pending ack-eliciting receipts in the matching packet number space.
expected_result: Focused tests prove that pending Initial and Handshake ACK state is prepended to live CRYPTO packets, the CRYPTO frame remains present after the ACK frame with its original stream offset and bytes, no ACK frame is invented when no ACK prefix is provided, and deterministic property coverage round-trips representative ACK-prefixed Initial and Handshake packet sizes. BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs validate the permanent long-header packet-protection benchmark additions without making public or MSQUIC comparison claims.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 S13P2P1-0010 requirement-home tests.
Run the full RFC 9000 S13P2P1 requirement-home tests for S13P2P1-0009, S13P2P1-0010, and S13P2P1-0012.
Run the QuicHandshakePacketProtectionBenchmarks suite with BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs and preserve logs and reports.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Run canonical SpecTrace JSON validation and preserve the output, including standing repository-wide residual failures if present.
preconditions: Initial packet protection is configured for the live Initial flush path.
Handshake packet protection material is available for the live Handshake flush path.
ACK-generation state has already recorded pending Initial or Handshake packet receipts before the CRYPTO packet is composed.
Retry/replay, retransmission, endpoint interop, public API, and general delayed-ACK timer behavior are outside this verification.

supplemental_sections.heading: Benchmark Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The benchmark evidence is an internal Incursa-only packet-builder suite for Initial and Handshake CRYPTO packet construction with and without an ACK prefix. It is not a public facade, System.Net.Quic, MSQUIC, HTTP/3, or interop comparison.

status_summary: Focused runtime, packet-builder, deterministic fuzz/property, benchmark, render/check, and diff-hygiene proof passed locally on 2026-04-25 for the bounded live Initial and Handshake ACK piggyback floor. Repository-wide SpecTrace JSON validation may still report standing residual Markdown and unresolved-reference backlog; the failure log is preserved as evidence rather than reclassified as a focused behavior failure.
